title: [Feature]: Evaluate WebSocket connection support
problem: Some NATS deployments expose WebSocket connectivity for constrained network environments. nats-sinks currently documents standard NATS connection modes but does not certify WebSocket connections. Supporting this mode requires understanding client behavior, TLS expectations, proxy behavior, and production suitability.
proposal: Evaluate WebSocket connection support through nats-py capabilities, configuration fields, TLS settings, and integration tests. The evaluation concluded that WebSocket transport is viable but should remain treated as accepted-by-validation and not yet production-certified until separate configuration guardrails, optional header support, and integration certification work are implemented.
users: Operators in environments where direct NATS TCP connectivity is unavailable, maintainers tracking NATS client compatibility, and security reviewers assessing proxy and TLS behavior.
delivery_semantics: Connection transport must not change commit-then-ACK behavior. Reconnect, timeout, and shutdown behavior must remain consistent with existing connection handling.
security: WebSocket transport must validate TLS by default, avoid insecure proxy assumptions, and redact connection configuration. Documentation must avoid real network details and must clearly state production caveats.
acceptance: WebSocket support is evaluated and documented.
Configuration validation behavior is described, including current ws:// and wss:// scheme acceptance.
Follow-up feature requests are created for certified implementation work.
Reconnect and proxy behavior are described for this transport.
tests: Validate backlog JSON, Markdown links, focused backlog and documentation tests, and scripts/check.sh. Implementation tests are tracked in follow-up feature requests.
documentation: Updated docs/websocket-connection-evaluation.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/security.md, docs/operations.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, docs/roadmap.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close as a completed evaluation after findings are posted and follow-up feature requests are created. Implementation work remains in the follow-up issues.
